fault_hypothesis: On line 67, the mean is computed by dividing by `window` instead of `len(tail)`. When the series is shorter than the window, `tail` has fewer elements than `window`, so dividing by `window` gives a wrong (too small) result instead of dividing by the actual number of samples used.

constraints.intent: After computing mean, verify it equals total divided by the actual tail length, not by window.
Check that mean times actual tail length equals total, catching division by wrong denominator.
Before returning mean, confirm it was computed using len(tail) as the divisor.

spec.expr: __cbfl_result is None or abs(__cbfl_result * min(len(values), window) - sum(values[-window:])) < 1e-9

constraints.intent: Returned mean times actual sample count should equal the sum of the tail slice.
